preamble: The shift from the alveolar stop [d] to the bilabial stop [b] is a classic example of regressive place assimilation. It occurs when the tongue's alveolar position for [d] is abandoned in favor of the lip-closure required for a following bilabial sound (like [p] or [b]).

examples.note: Synchronic assimilation in rapid, connected speech.
Historical prefix assimilation frozen into the spelling.
